Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. WebPKI and You 57 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Datahäxan 67 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. About memory pressure, lock contention, and Data-oriented Design 54 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 246 upvotes, 54 comments
  5. Printf-Tac-Toe 23 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 165 upvotes, 39 comments
  7. Temporal: The 9-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 685 upvotes, 216 comments
  8. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans 3589 upvotes, 1337 comments
  9. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 562 upvotes, 199 comments
  10. 1B identity records exposed in ID verification data leak 23 upvotes, 4 comments
  11. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 56 upvotes, 17 comments
  12. Show HN: s@: decentralized social networking over static sites 285 upvotes, 121 comments
  13. BitNet: Inference framework for 1-bit LLMs 341 upvotes, 163 comments
  14. Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs would not be merged 237 upvotes, 114 comments
  15. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  16. Reliable Software in the LLM Era 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. Faster asin() was hiding in plain sight 206 upvotes, 113 comments
  18. Google closes deal to acquire Wiz 296 upvotes, 172 comments
  19. SBCL: A Sanely-Bootstrappable Common Lisp (2008) [pdf] 47 upvotes, 20 comments
  20. Iran-backed hackers claim wiper attack on medtech firm Stryker 171 upvotes, 98 comments
  21. Show HN: A context-aware permission guard for Claude Code 103 upvotes, 56 comments
  22. Galaxy Zoo 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. Returning to Rails in 2026 131 upvotes, 78 comments
  24. NASA's DART spacecraft changed an asteroid's orbit around the sun 31 upvotes, 13 comments
  25. Entities enabling scientific fraud at scale (2025) 289 upvotes, 202 comments
  26. Personal Computer by Perplexity 161 upvotes, 126 comments
  27. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 311 upvotes, 282 comments
  28. I don't use LLMs for programming 39 upvotes, 29 comments
  29. The MacBook Neo 541 upvotes, 867 comments
  30. Meticulous (YC S21) is hiring to redefine software dev 0 upvotes, 0 comments
